title: Filter the Muddy Water
activity: Topic M2.S7.T12 — Filtering muddy water with filter paper (residue, filtrate, filtration)
description: A tap-and-select Class 6 guided experiment on filtration. In Explore (learn) mode the student folds a round filter paper into a cone (3 taps: half → quarter → open into the funnel), predicts whether the mud will pass through, then presses 'Pour' to tip muddy water into the cone and watch the mud build up as a dark band on the paper (the RESIDUE) while clear water drips down into the conical flask (the FILTRATE). A pore selector compares 'filter paper (fine pores)' vs 'loose cloth (coarse pores)': fine pores give clear filtrate, coarse cloth lets fine mud through so the filtrate stays cloudy — teaching that the choice of filter depends on particle size. The wrap states the rule (residue/filtrate names; filtration separates an INSOLUBLE solid from a liquid; finer pores → clearer filtrate). Quiz (practice) mode is a gamified round on the two names, what filtration separates, pore size, and everyday filters (cotton/charcoal/sand, tea bags), with a result popup (score count-up, stars, confetti) and a wrong-answer concept review. No drag-drop of cards anywhere — only taps and one pore selector.
prodSafe: No 'lucide-react' import — all icons are inline SVGs defined in the file, so the tool renders on every iframe-renderer version (an external icon name can never be undefined at runtime).

additionalProps.feedbackMessage: Press “Pour” to start filtering. Watch where the mud and clear water end up.
additionalProps.predictionPrompt: Will the mud pass THROUGH the filter paper, or be held back?

gradeLevel.description: Tier preset. 6 = heaviest gamification, story framing, biggest text, slowest/most dramatic pour, 70-particle confetti + 5 stars. 7 = moderate. 8 = light/formal. Authored for Class 6.

themeColor.description: Accent hint. The tool uses the Singularity palette: header purple, residue brown, filtrate teal, CTAs orange #FF7212.

enabledModes.description: Modes shown in the selector (Explore / Quiz).

showModeSelector.description: Show the Explore/Quiz toggle in the header.

showNavigation.description: Show Back/Next in Explore. Auto-hidden when filterSteps freezes one screen.

showPlayPause.description: Reserved; this interactive tool advances via Next/Back and in-stage taps, not host autoplay.

filterSteps.description: Freeze the Explore flow on ONE screen and hide the nav. Single-element array of S1..S4: S1=Fold the paper, S2=Predict, S3=Pour & filter, S4=Name the parts (wrap). Empty = full free-running flow.

autoPlayDuration.description: Host auto-advance hint, in ms. Ignored — the tool calls setStopAutoNext(true) because every screen needs interaction.

pore.description: Starting filter material. 'paper' = fine pores → clear filtrate; 'cloth' = coarse pores → cloudy filtrate (some fine mud slips through). The student can switch between them on the Pour screen to compare.

showHints.description: Show the small inline coaching hints / scene captions.

feedbackMessage.default: Press “Pour” to start filtering. Watch where the mud and clear water end up.
feedbackMessage.description: One-line coaching message shown under the apparatus before pouring.

predictionPrompt.default: Will the mud pass THROUGH the filter paper, or be held back?
predictionPrompt.description: Question shown on the Predict screen (S2).

liquidLabel.description: Name of the mixture being filtered. Re-theme for other insoluble-solid-in-liquid cases (e.g. 'tea with leaves', 'sandy water').

residueLabel.description: Name of the solid trapped on the paper (the residue), e.g. 'mud', 'sand', 'tea leaves'.

filtrateLabel.description: Name of the liquid that passes through (the filtrate).

muddyColor.description: Hex colour of the unfiltered (muddy) liquid and the pour stream.

residueColor.description: Hex colour of the trapped solid (residue) and the cloudiness tint when pores are coarse.

modes.label: Explore
modes.description: Fold the filter paper into a cone → predict whether mud passes → pour & watch mud stay (residue) while clear water drips into the flask (filtrate); compare filter paper vs loose cloth; then the rule wrap.

modes.label: Quiz
modes.description: Gamified 4-question round on residue vs filtrate, what filtration separates (insoluble solid from liquid), pore size → clarity, and everyday filters (cotton/charcoal/sand) → result popup → wrong-answer concept review → play again with a fresh shuffle.

learn.title: Fold the filter paper
learn.description: Tap 'Fold' three times to fold the round filter paper (half → quarter) then open it into a cone seated in the funnel. Gate: cone is ready.

learn.title: Predict the result
learn.description: Student predicts whether the mud will pass through the paper or be held back (tap-select). Gate: a prediction is made.

learn.title: Pour & filter
learn.description: Press 'Pour' to tip muddy water into the cone; mud builds up as residue on the paper while clear filtrate drips into the flask. A pore selector toggles filter paper (clear) vs loose cloth (cloudy). Gate: pour completes.

learn.title: Name the parts
learn.description: The labelled result: residue on the paper, filtrate in the flask. Closes with the filtration rule and a 'Take the Quiz' CTA.

practice.title: Quiz round
practice.description: 4 randomized questions: name the residue; name the filtrate; what filtration separates (insoluble solid from liquid); finer pores → clearer filtrate; everyday filters (cotton/charcoal/sand vs a dissolved solution). Silent play.

practice.title: Result
practice.description: Score count-up, 5 stars, 70-particle confetti, tier encouragement, CTAs.

practice.title: Review
practice.description: Wrong-answer cards with concept explanations; prev/next; play again.

interactionNotes: Tap/click + ONE pore selector — no drag-and-drop of cards (house rule; robust on mobile for young students).

The apparatus is a single responsive SVG side-view scene: tripod stand → glass funnel with a folded filter-paper cone → conical flask. Driven by foldStage (0..3), poured (0..1), residue (0..1), filtrate (0..1), clarity (0..1).

Pour animation is physically honest: only liquid drips through the stem; mud accumulates as a brown cap on the paper. Coarse cloth (clarity ~0.55) tints the filtrate cloudy; fine paper (clarity 1) leaves it clear.
Predict-then-reveal: residue and filtrate are hidden on the Predict screen until the student commits a guess, then pouring reveals where each ends up.
filterSteps S1..S4 freezes Explore on a single screen and hides Back/Next, for embedding one moment at a time alongside the lesson.
Calls setStopAutoNext(true) and reports setStepDetails on every step/phase change.
Responsive from 320px; touch targets 56px at Class 6; scene caps at 520px wide and scales down with the container.
PROD-SAFE ICONS: no lucide-react dependency — icons are inline SVGs, so no 'X is not defined' error regardless of the renderer's injected icon allowlist.
instructions_for_student: Fold the filter paper into a cone, predict whether the mud will pass through, then pour the muddy water and watch where the mud and the clear water end up.
